message:
fix(connectors, fcp-host): cron schedule validation, google-chat secret redaction, doctor diagnostic improvements

connectors/cron: Add schedule string validation before passing to
cron parser.

connectors/google-chat: Add manual Debug impl with [REDACTED] for
cached access token, consistent with other connectors.

fcp-host/doctor.rs: Improve diagnostic report formatting and error
message clarity.

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1902,7 +1902,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-sfa5w","title":"Set correct non-default timeouts for connectors with known latency profiles","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:32.916126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:59.489946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:59.489775Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","connectors","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-sfa5w","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6gtq7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:46.903866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1958,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-sfa5w","author":"jemanuel","text":"DEPENDS ON: manifest [timeouts] schema being available (6gtq7).

Connectors that need non-default timeouts (i.e., 30s is wrong):

LONG-RUNNING (need longer):
- anthropic: 120-300s for inference (client.rs:106 already uses 120s for HTTP, 300s for execution context — but these should come from manifest not code)
- openai: similar to anthropic, inference can take 60-120s
- google-ai: similar, generative AI responses can be slow
- whisper: audio transcription can take minutes for long files (MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES=25MB)
- terraform: plan/apply operations can take minutes
- kubernetes: apply/delete operations can take 60s+

SHORT-RUNNING (could use tighter):
- redis: key-value operations should complete in <5s
- elasticsearch: queries should complete in <15s
- duckdb: local queries should be fast

VARIABLE:
- browser: page navigation/screenshot can vary wildly (connector.rs already has 300s wall_clock_timeout_ms)
- s3: upload operations depend on file size

Steps:
1. For each connector above, add a [timeouts] section to manifest.toml with the correct values
2. Remove the hard-coded Duration::from_secs() from client.rs where it duplicates the manifest
3. Verify tests still pass with manifest-driven timeouts","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-shj4","title":"TEST: fcp-oauth Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-oauth Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for the OAuth2/OAuth1 authentication library.

## Test Categories

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
- [ ] Authorization URL generation with state/PKCE
- [ ] Token exchange with valid/invalid codes
- [ ] Refresh token flow
- [ ] Token expiration handling
- [ ] Scope validation

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Flow
- [ ] Token acquisition
- [ ] Caching and refresh

### OAuth 1.0a Flow (Twitter/legacy)
- [ ] Request token acquisition
- [ ] User authorization redirect
- [ ] Access token exchange
- [ ] Signature generation (HMAC-SHA1)

### Token Management
- [ ] Token storage/retrieval
- [ ] Token validation
- [ ] Secure token handling (no disk writes)

### Error Handling
- [ ] Invalid client credentials
- [ ] Expired tokens
- [ ] Revoked tokens
- [ ] Network failures
- [ ] Rate limits

## Mocking Strategy
- Use wiremock to mock OAuth provider endpoints
- Test against mock Google, GitHub, Twitter, Slack endpoints
- Inject errors at each stage of flows

## Coverage Target: >90%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:33.654746783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:36.615857990Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:36.615857990Z","close_reason":"OAuth functionality is connector-specific, not a platform crate. OAuth testing belongs in individual connector test suites.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-shj4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-shl11","title":"Workspace clippy sweep + fcp-raptorq DecodeError Display fix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T08:13:08.406338Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T08:13:14.759221Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T08:13:14.759204Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":751,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-shl11","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Fixed fcp-raptorq DecodeError missing Display (added thiserror::Error derive), fixed clippy across fcp-cbor, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-tailscale, fcp-sdk, fcp-twitter, fcp-llm-router, fcp-youtube, fcp-microsoft365, fcp-sentry, fcp-slack. All workspace clippy + tests clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T08:13:14Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","title":"[FCP3/P3.5] Add conformance vectors for replay, repair, evidence retrieval, and recovery flows","description":"Background: protocol and durable plane claims need executable proof. Replay, rekey, repair, evidence retrieval, and chunk recovery are exactly the kinds of flows that rot when they exist only as prose.

Scope: add conformance vectors plus scriptable end to end scenarios for replay, rekey, repair, durable explanation retrieval, and loss or recovery paths that touch fountain or chunk semantics where relevant. Require verbose FCPC and FCPS logs, durable object snapshots, and clear artifact bundles so failures can be debugged without reconstructing the scenario by hand.

Acceptance: there is repeatable automated evidence that the normative wire plane and durable plane remain aligned under both happy path and recovery scenarios, with detailed logs and artifacts for diagnosis.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoCompass","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.882500Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:19:17.340111Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-3","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:28.819716Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:27.790718Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:28.309606Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:27.288396Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2146,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Claiming the now-unblocked P3.5 conformance/e2e bead after closing ezkdk. Initial archaeology points to these main extension seams:
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mvp_vertical_slice.rs already has offline_repair and epoch_replay scenarios, but they currently only log phase transitions and checkpoint validity rather than asserting the new lifecycle / repair-evaluation / GC evidence bundle surfaces.
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs already has crash-recovery style scenarios and structured scenario logging, so it is a likely place to add richer recovery/evidence assertions.
- crates/fcp-e2e/src/evidence.rs and crates/fcp-e2e/src/host_e2e.rs already define evidence bundles and replay instructions, and crates/fcp-e2e/tests/offline_repair_e2e.rs now has the strongest durable artifact-bundle fixture to mine for downstream vectors.

Next step: tighten one focused conformance/evidence path rather than broadening the matrix immediately, then verify via rch from a compliant workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:35:48Z"},{"id":2155,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Implemented the first focused P3.5 slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/fcps_durability_boundary.rs: added a deterministic repair/recovery artifact-bundle conformance test that composes lifecycle snapshot, repair evaluation, repair planning, and GC transcript into one retrieval-friendly evidence bundle and proves it is stable across repeated runs plus JSON roundtrip retrieval.

Remote verification passed via rch on the reduced workspace after extending it with the missing conformance/path-dependency crates:
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test fcps_durability_boundary repair_recovery_artifact_bundle_is_deterministic_and_serializable -- --nocapture

Notable fix during verification: switched the new test from a field-literal ObjectTransmissionInfo constructor to ObjectTransmissionInfo::from_oti(ObjectTransmissionInformation::new(...)) so the test is resilient to the reduced-workspace store revision drift and matches the intended cross-crate contract more cleanly.

Bead remains in progress; next likely slice is to propagate comparable recovery/evidence assertions into the existing mvp_vertical_slice or integration_scenarios conformance paths rather than keeping this coverage isolated to the durability-boundary test.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:57:01Z"},{"id":2156,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Using bv robot triage to reassess next work: tbjdk remains the top global blocker, but it is already saturated with active narrow slices from SageDuck/BronzeCondor/Codex/OrangeMarsh. To avoid overlap, I am continuing the next actually-actionable high-impact bead I already own: si48n. Planned next slice: extend the existing conformance scenario harness (likely mvp_vertical_slice.rs and/or integration_scenarios.rs) so recovery paths assert structured artifact/evidence content rather than leaving the new repair/lifecycle/GC bundle semantics isolated to fcps_durability_boundary.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:00:03Z"},{"id":2157,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Added the next P3.5 scenario-level evidence slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mvp_vertical_slice.rs. offline_repair_reduced_availability_then_recovery now builds a typed recovery artifact bundle for contract.offline_repair_recovery, validates harness JSONL logs, asserts ordered phase coverage (distribute/failure/degraded/recovery), records degraded/recovered coverage in basis points, and proves the bundle survives JSON retrieval roundtrip. Remote verification is running via rch from the reduced verification workspace against cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test mvp_vertical_slice offline_repair_reduced_availability_then_recovery -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:04:42Z"},{"id":2158,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Remote verification passed for the new mvp/offline-repair evidence slice via the reduced rch workspace: cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test mvp_vertical_slice offline_repair_reduced_availability_then_recovery -- --nocapture. Worker vmi1152480 completed successfully with 1 passed, 0 failed. This keeps si48n moving by adding a scenario-level recovery artifact contract on top of the earlier durability-boundary bundle coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:08:30Z"},{"id":2159,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Fresh-eyes review of the new mvp offline-repair evidence slice found two real weaknesses and fixed them in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mvp_vertical_slice.rs: (1) the artifact bundle was hard-coding before/after node counts instead of recording observed state, and (2) the scenario treated node liveness as recovery instead of proving the restarted node actually regained the object. The test now counts actual available replicas before/after repair, records those counts in the bundle, and asserts the restarted node has the object after gossip repair. Remote re-verification through the reduced rch workspace is currently blocked by unrelated stale-crate drift in that auxiliary workspace (first fcp-raptorq/fcp-store, then stale fcp-registry), not by the new scenario assertions themselves.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:22:41Z"},{"id":2160,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cross-agent review pass found and fixed two correctness issues outside the original conformance slice: (1) crates/fcp-raptorq/src/codec/decoder.rs wavefront state was initialized over K instead of L, which could drop intermediate-column recovery paths; added a regression test that compares wavefront vs sequential decode on a repair-heavy scenario. (2) crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs treated malformed reconstructed control-plane candidates as timeouts and dropped pending state before validation; now malformed candidates are treated as incomplete and pending state is preserved so later repair symbols can still converge. Remote cargo re-verification is still blocked this turn because rch failed open with RCH-E326 from the main workspace, so I stopped the local fallback immediately and kept verification to static/rustfmt/git-diff checks.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:39:22Z"},{"id":2161,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fresh-eyes degraded transport audit: fixed a real reconstruction-state aliasing bug in crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs by including source symbol count k in PendingReconstructionKey and rejecting mixed-k CONTROL_PLANE frames before decode. Added regressions for mixed-k rejection and same-object/different-k pending separation. Non-cargo checks passed: rustfmt, git diff --check, and ubs --only=rust on degraded.rs (0 criticals). Targeted rch cargo verification hit RCH-E326 and attempted local fallback again, so I stopped at that boundary; no trusted cargo verification result yet.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:50:08Z"},{"id":2162,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cross-agent repair-planner review: fixed an explainability bug in crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs where over-budget repair candidates were silently dropped under ordinary cycle-budget exhaustion instead of being surfaced in plan.deferred. Added a new RepairReasonCode::DeferredCycleBudget and changed selection logic so skipped work remains visible unless it was specifically deferred for power/metered constraints. Updated integration coverage in crates/fcp-store/tests/store_repair_integration.rs to assert the omitted below-target object is reported as deferred and that actions+deferred matches object_count_below_target. Non-cargo checks passed: rustfmt and git diff --check. Targeted rch cargo verification hit RCH-E326 and attempted local fallback again; I interrupted the local cargo process immediately, so there is still no trusted cargo result for this fix.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:57:11Z"},{"id":2165,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cross-agent registry review found a trust-composition bug in crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs: verify_bundle() let publisher verification errors/threshold failures short-circuit before an independently valid registry signature was considered. Patched verification to summarize publisher and registry outcomes first, then decide so a trusted registry path can still authorize the bundle. Added regressions for (1) valid registry signature + unknown publisher entry and (2) valid registry signature + unmet publisher threshold. Non-cargo checks passed: rustfmt and git diff --check. Targeted rch cargo verification for the new tests hit RCH-E326 and attempted local fail-open, so I interrupted it immediately; no trusted remote cargo result yet.","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:30:49Z"},{"id":2183,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"CalmCondor taking a narrow non-overlapping assist slice on the active P3.5 conformance bead: inspect and, if still untouched, extend crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs with one focused recovery/evidence assertion path so the new artifact-bundle semantics are exercised outside fcps_durability_boundary.rs and mvp_vertical_slice.rs. I am explicitly staying off those two files and off the registry/store/mesh fixes already discussed in thread comments.","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:56:32Z"},{"id":2185,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"Completed a narrow non-overlapping assist slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs only. scenario_crash_recovery() now snapshots its own structured logs, validates JSONL, builds a typed CrashRecoveryArtifactBundle, and asserts retrieval-friendly JSON roundtrip plus ordered setup/verify assertion coverage; this extends the new recovery artifact semantics beyond fcps_durability_boundary.rs and mvp_vertical_slice.rs without touching those files. Local checks passed: rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check on the file, and ubs --only=rust on the file (0 criticals; remaining warnings are the expected test-file assert/unwrap inventory). Remote-only verification attempt used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test integration_scenarios scenario_crash_recovery -- --nocapture from repo root; worker vmi1227854 was selected and topology preflight succeeded, but the run expanded to full dirty 188-root closure sync across flywheel_connectors + asupersync. I interrupted it before remote cargo/local fallback so this slice does not yet have a trusted cargo result.","created_at":"2026-03-31T20:02:35Z"},{"id":2188,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"Taking one more narrow follow-on inside crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs only: extend scenario_degraded_symbol_availability() with a typed degraded-availability artifact bundle plus JSONL/retrieval assertions. Still staying off fcps_durability_boundary.rs, mvp_vertical_slice.rs, and all registry/store/mesh surfaces.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:00:02Z"},{"id":2191,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"Completed a second narrow same-file assist slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs. scenario_degraded_symbol_availability() now uses scenario constants, records the concrete crashed node id, snapshots its own structured logs, validates JSONL, builds a typed DegradedAvailabilityArtifactBundle, and asserts retrieval-friendly JSON roundtrip plus ordered setup/verify assertion coverage. I also factored the shared per-scenario assertion extraction used by the earlier crash-recovery bundle. Local checks passed: rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check on the file, and ubs --only=rust on the file (0 criticals; remaining warnings are the expected test-file assert/unwrap inventory). Crate-local remote verification also reached real remote cargo this time: from crates/fcp-conformance, RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo test --test integration_scenarios scenario_degraded_symbol_availability -- --nocapture selected worker vmi1149989, synced a 20-root closure, verified dependency preflight, then remote cargo failed only because worker vmi1149989 is missing nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. I interrupted the automatic local fallback immediately, so there is still no trusted local cargo result but this does classify the blocker as worker toolchain drift rather than a repo metadata/cycle failure.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:03:06Z"},{"id":2197,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"Taking one more same-file follow-on in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs only: extend scenario_partition_heal_convergence() with a typed partition-heal artifact bundle plus JSONL/retrieval assertions. Still staying off all other files and crates.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:08:45Z"},{"id":2198,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"CalmCondor","text":"Completed a third same-file assist slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs. scenario_partition_heal_convergence() now uses scenario constants, captures concrete node ids and partition duration, snapshots its own structured logs, validates JSONL, builds a typed PartitionHealArtifactBundle, and asserts retrieval-friendly JSON roundtrip plus ordered partition/heal/verify assertion coverage. The bundle records convergence status, pending message count, per-node object visibility, and whether the previously isolated node received both objects after heal. Local checks passed: rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check on the file, and ubs --only=rust on the file (0 criticals; remaining warnings are the expected test-file assert/unwrap inventory). So this slice has local static verification only; the remote blocker here was worker-preflight selection failure rather than a Rust diagnostic.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:45:45Z"},{"id":2215,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed another same-file retrieval-artifact slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs: scenario_multi_node_failure_within_tolerance now snapshots scenario logs, validates JSONL, builds a typed MultiNodeFailureArtifactBundle, and asserts JSON roundtrip plus ordered setup -> verify coverage. Still staying off all other files and crates.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:51:07Z"},{"id":2218,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed another same-file retrieval-artifact slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs: scenario_quorum_loss now uses scenario constants, snapshots scenario logs, validates JSONL, builds a typed QuorumLossArtifactBundle, and asserts JSON roundtrip plus ordered setup -> verify coverage. Still staying off all other files and crates.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:19:17Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","title":"[FCP3/P3.5] Add conformance vectors for replay, repair, evidence retrieval, and recovery flows","description":"Background: protocol and durable plane claims need executable proof. Replay, rekey, repair, evidence retrieval, and chunk recovery are exactly the kinds of flows that rot when they exist only as prose.

Scope: add conformance vectors plus scriptable end to end scenarios for replay, rekey, repair, durable explanation retrieval, and loss or recovery paths that touch fountain or chunk semantics where relevant. Require verbose FCPC and FCPS logs, durable object snapshots, and clear artifact bundles so failures can be debugged without reconstructing the scenario by hand.

Acceptance: there is repeatable automated evidence that the normative wire plane and durable plane remain aligned under both happy path and recovery scenarios, with detailed logs and artifacts for diagnosis.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoCompass","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.882500Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:22:45.385089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-3","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:28.819716Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:27.790718Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:28.309606Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:27.288396Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2146,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Claiming the now-unblocked P3.5 conformance/e2e bead after closing ezkdk. Initial archaeology points to these main extension seams:
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mvp_vertical_slice.rs already has offline_repair and epoch_replay scenarios, but they currently only log phase transitions and checkpoint validity rather than asserting the new lifecycle / repair-evaluation / GC evidence bundle surfaces.
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/integration_scenarios.rs already has crash-recovery style scenarios and structured scenario logging, so it is a likely place to add richer recovery/evidence assertions.
- crates/fcp-e2e/src/evidence.rs and crates/fcp-e2e/src/host_e2e.rs already define evidence bundles and replay instructions, and crates/fcp-e2e/tests/offline_repair_e2e.rs now has the strongest durable artifact-bundle fixture to mine for downstream vectors.

Next step: tighten one focused conformance/evidence path rather than broadening the matrix immediately, then verify via rch from a compliant workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:35:48Z"},{"id":2155,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Implemented the first focused P3.5 slice in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/fcps_durability_boundary.rs: added a deterministic repair/recovery artifact-bundle conformance test that composes lifecycle snapshot, repair evaluation, repair planning, and GC transcript into one retrieval-friendly evidence bundle and proves it is stable across repeated runs plus JSON roundtrip retrieval.

Remote verification passed via rch on the reduced workspace after extending it with the missing conformance/path-dependency crates:
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test fcps_durability_boundary repair_recovery_artifact_bundle_is_deterministic_and_serializable -- --nocapture

Notable fix during verification: switched the new test from a field-literal ObjectTransmissionInfo constructor to ObjectTransmissionInfo::from_oti(ObjectTransmissionInformation::new(...)) so the test is resilient to the reduced-workspace store revision drift and matches the intended cross-crate contract more cleanly.

Bead remains in progress; next likely slice is to propagate comparable recovery/evidence assertions into the existing mvp_vertical_slice or integration_scenarios conformance paths rather than keeping this coverage isolated to the durability-boundary test.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:57:01Z"},{"id":2156,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Using bv robot triage to reassess next work: tbjdk remains the top global blocker, but it is already saturated with active narrow slices from SageDuck/BronzeCondor/Codex/OrangeMarsh. This keeps si48n moving by adding a scenario-level recovery artifact contract on top of the earlier durability-boundary bundle coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:08:30Z"},{"id":2159,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"IndigoCompass","text":"Fresh-eyes review of the new mvp offline-repair evidence slice found two real weaknesses and fixed them in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mvp_vertical_slice.rs: (1) the artifact bundle was hard-coding before/after node counts instead of recording observed state, and (2) the scenario treated node liveness as recovery instead of proving the restarted node actually regained the object. (2) crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs treated malformed reconstructed control-plane candidates as timeouts and dropped pending state before validation; now malformed candidates are treated as incomplete and pending state is preserved so later repair symbols can still converge. Remote cargo re-verification is still blocked this turn because rch failed open with RCH-E326 from the main workspace, so I stopped the local fallback immediately and kept verification to static/rustfmt/git-diff checks.","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:39:22Z"},{"id":2161,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fresh-eyes degraded transport audit: fixed a real reconstruction-state aliasing bug in crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs by including source symbol count k in PendingReconstructionKey and rejecting mixed-k CONTROL_PLANE frames before decode.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","title":"[FCP3/P6] Move CLI and operator truth onto mesh backed contracts without fakery","description":"Background: fwc currently remains truthful by reading a host first world. That is correct today, but V3 wants operator surfaces to reflect mesh backed truth once the underlying layers can actually provide it.

Scope: replace CLI reach through into broad semantic crates with stable contracts, preserve explicit provenance and fallback signaling, rewrite operator facing docs and commands so they reflect evidence backed mesh reality rather than host local special cases, and preserve a pre cutover benchmark and transcript baseline so phase 7 proof can compare against the last truthful transitional state instead of memory. This phase must also lock in machine readable contract snapshots, acceptance test scripts, before and after transcripts, detailed structured logs, and explicit ties to the unit tests and e2e test scripts provided by the underlying implementation beads so user visible behavior stays auditable while the truth source moves underneath it. This phase is complete when the CLI becomes mesh first because the system underneath it is mesh first and the operator surface has reproducible artifacts that show the transition honestly.

Acceptance: the CLI and operator surface become mesh first without fakery, retain explicit provenance semantics, preserve script friendly and human friendly logs and diagnostics, and hand phase 7 a preserved pre deletion baseline bundle for comparison and proof.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:02.528732Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:50:37.073992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","mesh-first","operator","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:13:09.066248Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:15.950987Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:15.045446Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kpj16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:15.275752Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:15.488549Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:15.733688Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2053,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Do not make the CLI sound more mesh native ahead of reality. The operator surface should become mesh first only by consuming better evidence backed contracts, while still being explicit about offline, host local, and mesh backed provenance states.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","title":"fcp.airtable: Flexible Database and Spreadsheet Connector","description":"# fcp.airtable - Flexible Database and Spreadsheet Connector (FCP2)

## Success Criteria
- `flywheel_connectors-soft.1` (Manifest + Capability Map) is complete and passes static validation (IDs, schemas, NetworkConstraints, safety tiers).
- `flywheel_connectors-soft.2` (Provisioning Automation) is complete, minimizes human prompts, and is fully mockable for tests.
- `flywheel_connectors-soft.3` (Unit/Integration Tests) are mock-only, include success + denial paths, and assert log redaction (no secrets/PII).
- `flywheel_connectors-soft.4` (E2E Compliance Run) passes the shared compliance runner and emits an evidence bundle (logs + receipts + audit checks).
- Connector is V2-only: single-zone binding, default deny, constrained egress, audited risky/dangerous ops.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 86/100** — High priority Tier 2 connector.

**Why Critical**: Airtable is the **leading no-code database platform**, bridging spreadsheets and databases. Widely used by operations teams, PMs, and non-technical users. Enables agents to work with structured data managed by business users.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Business User Data)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Storage,      // Record storage
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // CRUD operations
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Storage**: Record storage and retrieval
- **Operational**: CRUD, filtering, sorting
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for record changes

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Pagination cursors, sync state
- State tracked: `{ offsets: HashMap<TableId, Offset>, webhook_state: WebhookState }`
- Lease required for writer fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Airtable API endpoints
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.airtable.com".into(),
        // For file attachments
        "dl.airtable.com".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Record data may contain user input
record_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Attachment URLs are external
attachment.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `airtable.records.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `airtable.schema.read` | Safe | Metadata only |
| `airtable.records.create` | Risky | Creates data |
| `airtable.records.update` | Risky | Modifies data |
| `airtable.records.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible |
| `airtable.tables.create` | Dangerous | Schema changes |
| `airtable.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Event routing |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Data Operations
- CRUD on records across bases and tables
- Complex filtering and sorting
- Field type handling (attachments, linked records, formulas)
- View-based data access

### Schema Operations
- Read table/field schemas
- Understand relationships between tables
- Access formula field definitions

### Automation Support
- Webhook triggers on record changes
- Bulk record operations
- Sync with external systems

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Add this item to the inventory table"
"Find all tasks due this week"
"Update the status of this record"
"Get all records linked to this project"
"Export data from this view"
"Create a new record with attachments"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Stripe/Plaid Integration
**Financial tracking:**
- Transaction records in Airtable
- Budget tracking
- Invoice management

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Task mirroring
- Cross-platform project tracking

### Agent Mail Integration
- Record updates → agent threads
- Data change notifications

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API**: Primary CRUD operations
- **Metadata API**: Schema information
- **Webhooks**: Change notifications

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| Personal Access Token | Individual access |
| OAuth 2.0 | Integration apps |

### Rate Limits
```rust
RateLimits {
    requests_per_second: 5,
    // Per base limits
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:data:airtable:base:<id>` | Base-scoped | Single base |
| `z:data:airtable:full` | Full access | All bases |
| `z:data:airtable:readonly` | Read-only | Safe operations |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `storage` — Record storage
- `operational` — CRUD operations
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.airtable.com:443`
- `dl.airtable.com:443` (attachments)

### Credential Injection
- API tokens via egress proxy
- OAuth tokens via CredentialId

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `airtable.records.read` — List/get records
- `airtable.schema.read` — Table/field metadata
- `airtable.views.read` — View data

### Write Operations
- `airtable.records.create` — Create records
- `airtable.records.update` — Update records

### Admin Operations
- `airtable.records.delete` — Delete records (Dangerous)
- `airtable.tables.create` — Create tables (Dangerous)
- `airtable.webhooks.manage` — Configure webhooks (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### List Records
```
GET /v0/{baseId}/{tableIdOrName}?filterByFormula={status}="Active"
```

### Create Record
```json
POST /v0/{baseId}/{tableIdOrName}
{
  "fields": {
    "Name": "New Item",
    "Status": "Active",
    "Due Date": "2024-01-15"
  }
}
```

### Update Record
```json
PATCH /v0/{baseId}/{tableIdOrName}/{recordId}
{
  "fields": {
    "Status": "Completed"
  }
}
```

### Delete Record
```
DELETE /v0/{baseId}/{tableIdOrName}/{recordId}
```

---

## Field Type Handling

### Supported Types
- Text, Number, Date
- Single/Multiple Select
- Linked Records
- Attachments
- Formulas (read-only)
- Rollups (read-only)

### Attachment Handling
```json
{
  "Attachments": [
    {
      "url": "https://dl.airtable.com/...",
      "filename": "document.pdf"
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- Filter formula building
- Field type handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Airtable API responses
- Multi-table scenarios
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Airtable API calls in unit tests
- Record CRUD works
- Filter formulas work
- Linked records handled
- Attachments work
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","title":"fcp.airtable: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.airtable: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Lock down the Airtable connector’s **mechanical contract** so the platform can sandbox, authorize, and test it deterministically.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes: `storage`, `operational`, `streaming`.
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - allow Airtable API host(s)
  - explicitly handle attachment download hosts (enumerate/pin; HTTPS-only; bounded)
- State model declaration for webhooks (if webhook cursors/subscription state is persisted).

## Outputs
- Complete manifest spec stored in this bead.
- Good/bad manifest examples for static compliance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.
- Static compliance runner has enough detail to reject unsafe NetworkConstraints.
